entries.title: BORED APE YACHT CLUB | Website Redesign

messages.title: Technical Program Manager, Knowledge Systems
messages.message:
I already know the surges. I was Production Lead for Base at Coinbase, so this world isn't new to me. What's newer, and more relevant here, is the AI-assisted work I've been building lately.

The core problem with AI-assisted work isn't output, it's that the output scatters. Decisions in one doc, tasks with no context, conversations that go nowhere. So I built the operating system before accelerating the work: source of truth, workflow patterns, an orchestration agent that keeps everything connected. That's P.H.R.E.D., a Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery: https://lab26.randallfransen.com/phred

That's the same muscle Knowledge Systems needs: structure that makes AI actually useful and keeps humans in control of what can't be undone. Fifteen years of running cross-functional programs behind it.

Open to a call this week?

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery

messages.title: Staff Technical Program Manager, CX Agent Experience
messages.message:
I've already produced inside Coinbase, running production for Base as a contractor, so the compliance-heavy, high-stakes internal-tools world isn't new terrain for me. Owning delivery on a platform like Agent Cockpit is exactly the seat I like: intake, prioritization, dependency coordination, launch readiness, and being the one person keeping technical stakeholders aligned when critical-path items stall.

Most of my career has been that. At Swift I ran a $14MM HTC retainer with a team of 4. At AKQA I ran programs of 3-4 clients with senior direct reports. I also design AI workflows that keep human oversight in the loop instead of letting output scatter, which is half the battle right now.

Worth a call about the CX Agent Experience role?

messages.title: Brand Producer
messages.location: San Francisco, CA • New York, NY • United States
messages.message:
I've spent 15+ years being the producer who walks into a fast-moving creative team and quietly gets everyone pointed the same direction. Brand Studio work lives or dies on that: clean timelines, clear dependencies, freelancers and agencies onboarded without drama, and creatives left alone to make the good stuff.

A few reps that map to this one: Creative Producer on the BAYC.com redesign at Yuga, leading internal teams plus an outside studio (https://www.randallfransen.com/work/bored-ape-yacht-club-website-redesign), Executive Producer on Airbnb's global AirCover launch, and a $14MM HTC retainer at Swift with a lean team of 4. Web and paid campaign work, both in my wheelhouse.

Worth a call to see if I'd fit your Brand Producer seat?

insertedExample.oneHitter: Yuga’s property Bored Ape Yacht Club was still rocking it’s original design and functionality in 2023.

workExampleSelection.title: BORED APE YACHT CLUB | Website Redesign

messages.title: Senior Programs & Business Operations Lead, Airbnb Services
messages.message:
I ran the AirCover for Hosts global launch as EP at Airbnb, so I've already lived the concept-to-launch grind inside your org: cross-functional targets, no direct authority, operational readiness as the whole ballgame. This role is that muscle again, pointed at new Services categories.

Building programs from scratch is where I spend most of my time. At AKQA I stood up programs of 3-4 clients with senior direct reports; at Swift I ran a $14MM retainer with a lean team of 4. I jump in mid-stride and get the plumbing working before things scale.

One straight flag: I'm not a daily SQL hand. I read data and build the models around it, but I lean on partners for the heavy query work.

Worth a call to walk through the AirCover launch?

messages.title: Product Operations Manager
messages.message:
The part of this job that grabbed me: agentic workflows that keep launch work connected instead of scattered. That's the exact problem I've been solving. I built P.H.R.E.D., an operating system for AI-assisted production, because on a small team with an aggressive timeline the output wasn't the issue, it was that everything landed in different docs with no context. Here's the writeup: https://lab26.randallfransen.com/phred

Most of my 15+ years is Executive Producer work: coordinating end-to-end launches across cross-functional teams, from the AirBnB AirCover global launch to a Zero Knowledge Proof product at ALEO. Bringing structure to ambiguous, fast-moving projects is the thread through all of it.

Want to talk through how I'd approach launch ops at Notion's scale?

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery

messages.title: Creative Director, Growth
messages.message:
I've spent 15+ years running creative and production for teams that had to move fast without dropping the craft, and this Growth CD role reads like the exact seat I keep landing in. I directed agencies and internal teams on the BAYC.com redesign at Yuga Labs, partnering with a French studio, Make Me Pulse, to rebuild the front end and wallet functionality (https://www.randallfransen.com/work/bored-ape-yacht-club-website-redesign).

I've also produced launches at the frontier: AirCover at Airbnb, a Zero Knowledge Proof product at Aleo. New tech, real audiences, tight timelines. Lately I've been building AI-assisted workflows so speed doesn't scatter the work.

Can we grab 20 minutes to talk through how I'd steer the Growth creative bar?

insertedExample.oneHitter: Yuga’s property Bored Ape Yacht Club was still rocking it’s original design and functionality in 2023.

messages.title: Marketing Events Producer
messages.location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY
messages.message:
I've spent 15+ years running end-to-end digital and live production, and calling the show when a keynote or launch has to land in front of the world. On the AirBnB AirCover for Hosts global launch I was the Executive Producer coordinating the run-of-show, the vendors, and the real-time calls that keep a big moment from wobbling on stage.

What I'm best at: bringing process, wrangling vendors, and keeping a crew calm and on task while the clock runs. Managed a $14MM retainer with a team of 4 at Swift, and $4MM+ in production budgets at Crispin Porter + Bogusky, so scope, riders, and contracts are familiar ground.

Want me to walk you through how I'd run one of Anthropic's tentpole events?

messages.title: Strategy & Operations Manager, Consumer Revenue
messages.message:
I've spent 15 years standing up structure where none existed, usually mid-project, usually with the timeline already on fire. That's the part of this Consumer Revenue role I want.

Managed a $14MM HTC retainer at Swift with a lean team of 4, so I know how to build the reporting, guardrails, and approval frameworks that keep deals moving instead of leaking. At Coinbase I ran production for Base, so subscription-and-partnership economics in a platform world aren't foreign territory. And I build AI into the workflow rather than bolting it on: I put together an orchestration system, P.H.R.E.D., that keeps AI-assisted work connected instead of scattered across a dozen docs (https://lab26.randallfransen.com/phred).

Worth 20 minutes to see if I'm the person for your deal desk?

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery

messages.title: Professional Services Program Manager
messages.message:
I've spent 15+ years running exactly this kind of work: multi-workstream engagements for big clients where the real job is keeping people, plans, and dependencies from drifting apart. At Swift I ran a $14MM HTC retainer with a team of 4; at AKQA I ran programs of 3-4 clients with senior direct reports and won 4 new accounts over 2 years. Billable, external, client-facing. That's my native habitat.

Worth noting on the AI angle: I built P.H.R.E.D., a project OS that keeps AI-assisted work connected instead of scattered across a dozen docs. https://lab26.randallfransen.com/phred. GitLab expects AI in daily workflows, so I'd bring that in from day one, not bolt it on later.

Can we grab 20 minutes to talk through your largest engagements?

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery

messages.title: Viral Creative Producer
messages.message:
The Brian Baumgartner glass box is the kind of thing I'd have loved to run point on. I've spent 15+ years producing exactly this kind of high-stakes, aggressive-timeline work end to end, from a $14MM social account at Swift to AirBnB's global AirCover launch.

My last few years have been Web3 and brand activations, including a Gen Z activation for Mozilla built to punch up at Big Tech in a way only Firefox could: concept to IRL to distribution over a three-month run. https://www.randallfransen.com/work/billionaire-blast-off-mozilla

I know how to run talent, vendors, permits, and budget without letting the good idea die in the details. Your backlog of bigger swings is the part I'd want to hear about. Can we talk?

insertedExample.oneHitter: Create a Gen Z-coded moment to amplify Open What You Want.

messages.title: Head of Copy, Creative Studio
messages.location: New York City, NY; San Francisco, CA
messages.message:
I've spent 15+ years running creative production, and the part of this role I'd chase hardest is the connective tissue bit: keeping human creative vision and AI execution actually talking to each other. I built exactly that on a confidential project. P.H.R.E.D., my Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery: https://lab26.randallfransen.com/phred. It's an operating system that gives AI tools enough structure to help without the output scattering into a dozen dead docs, while humans keep control of scope and the calls you can't undo.

Before that I led the BAYC.com redesign at Yuga and ran a $14MM retainer with a team at Swift. I know how to build a bar and keep a team of strong voices working at speed.

Open to a call about the Head of Copy role?

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery

messages.title: Chief of Staff, to the CPO
messages.message:
My whole career has been jumping in mid-stride to connect strategy to execution: keeping leadership aligned, keeping the operating cadence humming, keeping the right priorities in front of the right people. As Program Director at AKQA I ran programs of 3-4 clients with 2-5 senior direct reports, and I managed a $14MM retainer with a team of 4 as Executive Producer at Swift.

The AI-first piece is where I've actually been living lately. I built P.H.R.E.D., an operating system for AI-assisted work so the output stops scattering across a dozen docs and actually connects: https://lab26.randallfransen.com/phred. Same instinct your CPO needs, pointed at a People org instead of a product team.

Worth a call to see if I fit what you're building?
